title: Cisco ISE Data Connect — DB-Direct Query Audit and Slow-Query Detection

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 10.2.2 — audit logging of admin actions; Data Connect direct DB access requires logging.
ITGC logical access — DB-direct access is privileged.

compliance.controlObjective: Database-access logging evidence for ISE Data Connect read-only consumers.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Run a deliberately heavy query via Data Connect (`SELECT * FROM mnt_radius_auth WHERE rownum > 1000000`). Query takes 60s. UC-17.1.58 fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Run a normal query that completes in 2s. UC-17.1.58 does not fire.

dataSources: ISE Data Connect (introduced ISE 3.2) provides direct read-only Oracle connectivity to MnT data. Audit/connection events emitted in *Administrative and Operational Audit* category — codes `52100` (Data Connect login), `52101` (Data Connect login failure), `52110` (Data Connect slow query). Query details may be captured at the consumer side (e.g. Splunk DB Connect) — `index=splunkdbx sourcetype=splunkdbx:db_query`. ISE OpenAPI exposes Data Connect status via `/api/v1/dataconnect/status`. Field: `db_user`, `query_duration_ms`, `query_text`.
app: `Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services` ([Splunkbase 1915](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1915)) + `Splunk DB Connect` for the consumer side.
spl:
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (52100, 52101, 52110))
  OR (index=splunkdbx sourcetype="splunkdbx:db_query" "jdbc:oracle:thin:@//ise-mnt:2484")
| stats count, count(eval(MessageCode=="52101")) as login_failures, count(eval(MessageCode=="52110" OR query_duration_ms > 30000)) as slow_queries, max(query_duration_ms) as max_duration by db_user
| where login_failures > 5 OR slow_queries > 0 OR max_duration > 60000
description: Audits Data Connect direct DB access events and detects login failures (potential brute-force) AND slow queries (>30s) that may degrade ISE platform performance.
value: Data Connect provides huge analytical value but exposes the MnT Oracle DB directly. Brute-force on the DB account or runaway slow queries can degrade the platform. Auditing protects the integration value while bounding the risk.
implementation: Confirm *Administrative and Operational Audit* at INFO. If using Splunk DB Connect, ensure DB Connect is logging query details. Schedule every 30 min.
visualization: (1) Single value: slow queries last 1h. (2) Top-N: queries by duration. (3) Login-failure trend.
knownFalsePositives: (a) **Initial deployment query** — Initial schema discovery may produce slow queries.  (b) **Quarterly audit-extract query** — Large audit-data extracts can legitimately take minutes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• ISE Data Connect enabled (Administration > System > Settings > Data Connect).
• Splunk DB Connect or another consumer.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *Administrative and Operational Audit* at INFO.

Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 30 min.

Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — Data Connect Audit":
• Slow-query count.
• Top-N queries.
• Login-failure trend.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Slow queries from analytics workload** — Coordinate to schedule outside peak.
• **Login failures from one host** — Brute-force or stale credential cache.
• **No DB Connect events** — DB Connect not configured to log queries.
grandmaExplanation: ISE allows a special direct-to-database connection for big reports. We watch who's connecting and how long their queries take — slow queries can slow down ISE itself, and many failed logins can mean someone's trying to crack a password.
